free_text: Neutron elastic scattering from Mo-92,96,98,100 between
neutron energies of 7 to 26-MeV
11-MeV tandem van de Graaff
Protons on tritium for 7-MeV neutrons
Deuterons on deuterium for 9 and 11-MeV neutrons
Deuterons on tritium for 20 and 26-MeV neutrons
All samples enriched to better than 95-percent. 2-cm
DIAMTER by 2-cm long containing approx 2/3 mole. Has
oxygen contamination.
5-cm thick by 18.4-cm diameter liquid scintilla-
tor with photomultiplier.
5-cm thick by 5-cm diameter liquid scintillator to
monitor neutron flux
Normalized at zero degrees using Ne-224 neutron
monitor
Pulsed beam time of flight with 3.7-meter path
length for 7-MeV neutrons and 6.6-meter for all
others. Pulse shape discrimination used to eliminate
gamma events. Samples suspended by light thread
11.8-cm from neutron source. Where inelastic peaks
could not Be resolved from elastic peaks theoretical
calculations of inelastic contribution was subtracted.
oxygen contribution obtained with forward angle scat-
tering from water fitted to optical model parameters.
Extracted elastic yields corrected for background, dead
time, source anisotropy, and relative detector effi-
ciency.
Angular distributions corrected for finite angular
geometry, multiple scattering, and neutron flux atten-
uation.
Oxygen content correction, maximum of 2-percent
compound nucleus contributions are negligible.
Overall uncertainty generally less than 10%
but as high as 20% for some backward angles.
Normalization error approx 5%
Scattering angle error approx 0.3 degrees
Approved by J.Rapaport, 79/4/20.
Data received from J.Rapaport, 23 Feb. 1979
OS. Converted to lower case, minor BIB
changes, ERR-ANALYS modified
Data for 9-MeV assigned larger error due to equipment
malfunction
Data for 9-MeV assigned larger error due to equipment
malfunction
Data for 9-MeV assigned larger error due to equipment
malfunction
